chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Etiquettes of the Fasting Person, What Invalidates the Fast, and What Does Not Invalidate it

parts.text: سَأَلَ حَنَانُ بْنُ سَدِيرٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ :

text: اَلْمَاءِ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ وَ لَكِنْ لاَ يَغْمِسْ وَ اَلْمَرْأَةُ لاَ تَسْتَنْقِعُ فِي اَلْمَاءِ لِأَنَّهَا تَحْمِلُ اَلْمَاءَ بِقُبُلِهَا».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1883 - Hanan ibn Sadir asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a fasting person immersing himself in water.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm, but he should not submerge his head. As for a woman, she should not immerse herself in water because it may carry water into her private part."
